title: Sweet Sticky Norwegian Sugar Pancakes
description: A traditional Norwegian dish featuring light brown soft sugar and shio-koji cure, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning. These sweet and gooey pancakes are kosher-friendly and sure to satisfy your sweet tooth.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, light brown soft sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract.
Add the shio-koji cure mixture to the wet ingredients and whisk until smooth.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until just combined.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat.
Drop the batter by 1/4 cupfuls onto the skillet or griddle.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancakes and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Serve the pancakes warm, drizzled with additional light brown soft sugar if desired.
